msgs.text:
According to the current attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 112 is again an attachment-only continuation: the required current-turn files are present, and there are still no independent measured nonlinear Certificate D artifacts attached beyond the history ledger itself.

Turn 112 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state remains unchanged:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The problem asks whether there exists a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓHwithπH∘Φ=πS,i.e. an isomorphism over the fixed quotient G. The attached sweep records the stronger statement that there is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G→F such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends toΓS=F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩=ΓH.This is exactly the requested over-G isomorphism.No new structural computation is warranted. The frozen proof chain remainsH1 -Nielsen equivalence+residual restricted-Lie induction+normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+Certificates D.1–D.4+finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The compactness descent is still the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms; each finite datum gives a finite over-G automorphism because the first layer is invertible and the finite Frattini criterion applies; a compatible inverse limit gives Φ:F→F, and intersecting all PN,J gives Φ(rS−1)=rH.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments contain the prompt, problem statement, and turn history, but no object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs independent of the ledger. Thus the strict gate is still

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. The latest history explicitly separates the theorem-level conclusion from the strict measured-output gate: the proof state remains ΓS≅GΓH, while the publication-grade measured Certificate D gate remains unopened until genuine measured artifacts are supplied.

So the attachment-only transition rule applies again:{mega_prompt,problem_statement,history only}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT)with no reopening of degree 16, the A2 -layer, the t-layer, the Koszul–Witt coefficient tower, or any Certificate D subcase.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 112 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 111. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN112-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
